title: Einstein equation and parity
summary: Packages the equation Ric(g₊) = −ng₊ together with a formal hᵣ-expansion whose odd coefficients below order n vanish.

mathematicalContent.heading: Poincaré–Einstein equation
mathematicalContent.body: For a bulk of dimension n+1 with n-dimensional conformal infinity, the stored Ricci symbol is required to equal −n times the metric.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal expansion and parity
mathematicalContent.body: The boundary family is represented by coefficients h₍ₘ₎. The formal parity condition says that every odd coefficient of degree m<n vanishes; thus the expansion is even through the orders strictly below the boundary dimension.

mathematicalContent.heading: Certificate boundary
mathematicalContent.body: Lean does not run the Einstein recursion here. IsPoincareEinstein stores the Einstein equation, the coefficient family, its parity law, the initial coefficient h₍₀₎=h₀, and an uninterpreted compatibility certificate.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores formal coefficients h₍ₘ₎ of the boundary metric family, the vanishing of all odd h₍ₘ₎ with m<n, and a proposition intended to express trace-freeness of the critical obstruction coefficient.
correspondence.leanType: structure PEFormalExpansion (X : PESpace) where coeff; even_to_order; tracefree_obstruction

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines evenness through order N to mean that every odd-indexed coefficient with index strictly less than N is zero.
correspondence.leanType: def HasEvenExpansionToOrder (N : Nat) : Prop := forall m, m < N -> Odd m -> E.coeff m = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Specializes the parity field of PEFormalExpansion to N=n, the boundary dimension. The proof is direct projection of even_to_order.

Certifies a normal-form package by storing the Einstein equation Ric(g₊)=−ng₊, a formal coefficient expansion, the initial value h₍₀₎=h₀, and a proposition intended to link that coefficient family to hᵣ.
correspondence.leanType: structure IsPoincareEinstein (NF : PoincareNormalFormData X) where einstein_equation; formalExpansion; coeff_zero; expansion_certificate

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Given an IsPoincareEinstein certificate, extracts its stored conclusion that odd coefficients below the boundary dimension vanish.

references.note: Background for the Poincaré–Einstein equation, normal-form expansion, and evenness phenomenon.

limitations: The equation and parity results are verified only under the IsPoincareEinstein and PEFormalExpansion interfaces. Ricci is a formal MetricSymbol, expansion_certificate and tracefree_obstruction are bare propositions, and no Einstein recursion or obstruction trace calculation is carried out in this file.
